ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the service provider */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL of the service provider */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Type of service provided (e.g., tax filing, health insurance) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the service provider */,
  "security_policy_url" TEXT /* URL to the service provider's security policy document. */,
  "last_audit_date" TEXT /* Date of the last security audit conducted on the service provider. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_providers_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES service_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the service type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service type */,
  "security_risk_level" TEXT /* Security risk level of the service type (e.g., low, medium, high). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerabilities" (
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vulnerability */,
  "vuln_name" TEXT /* Name of the vulnerability */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the vulnerability */,
  "date_discovered" TEXT /* Date the vulnerability was discovered */,
  "cvss_score" REAL /* CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System) score of the vulnerability */,
  "exploit_availability" INTEGER /* Whether an exploit for the vulnerability is publicly available. */,
  "exploit_complexity" TEXT /* Complexity of the exploit (e.g., low, medium, high). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provider_vulnerability_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "affected" INTEGER /* Whether the provider is affected by the vulnerability */,
  "patched" INTEGER /* Whether the provider has patched the vulnerability */,
  "change_password" INTEGER /* Whether users need to change their passwords */,
  "statement" TEXT /* Statement from the provider regarding the vulnerability */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the status was last updated. */,
  "status_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the status. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_vulnerability_status_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_vulnerability_status_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patches" (
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patch */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "patch_date" TEXT /* Date the patch was applied */,
  "patch_version" TEXT /* Version of the patch */,
  "patch_description" TEXT /* Description of the patch */,
  "patch_effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of the patch (e.g., partial, full). */,
  "patch_tested" INTEGER /* Whether the patch has been tested. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user action */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "action_required" TEXT /* Action required by users (e.g., change password) */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action required */,
  "action_completed" INTEGER /* Whether the required action has been completed by the user. */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date the action was completed. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communications" (
  "comm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "comm_date" TEXT /* Date the communication was made */,
  "comm_type" TEXT /* Type of communication (e.g., press release, email) */,
  "comm_content" TEXT /* Content of the communication */,
  "communication_status" TEXT /* Status of the communication (e.g., sent, received, read). */,
  "response_required" INTEGER /* Whether a response is required from the recipient. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., security analyst, data analyst, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login. */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed. */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for the action. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_assessments" (
  "assessment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security assessment */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "assessment_date" TEXT /* Date the assessment was conducted */,
  "assessor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the security assessor */,
  "assessment_details" TEXT /* Detailed findings of the security assessment */,
  "assessment_status" TEXT /* Status of the assessment (e.g., in progress, completed, pending). */,
  "assessment_rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the security assessment (e.g., 1-5). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assessment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_assessments_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_assessments_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_assessments_assessor_id FOREIGN KEY ("assessor_id") REFERENCES assessors ("assessor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assessors" (
  "assessor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security assessor */,
  "assessor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the security assessor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the security assessor */,
  "expertise" TEXT /* Area of expertise (e.g., cryptography, network security) */,
  "last_training_date" TEXT /* Date of the assessor's last training. */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification held by the assessor (e.g., CISSP, CEH). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assessor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerability_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vulnerability version */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the vulnerability */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the vulnerability version */,
  "patch_effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of the patch in this version (e.g., partial, full). */,
  "patch_tested" INTEGER /* Whether the patch has been tested in this version. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerability_versions_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provider_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider version */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provider */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the software */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the software version */,
  "security_compliance" TEXT /* Security compliance level of the software version (e.g., PCI-DSS, HIPAA). */,
  "security_features" TEXT /* List of security features included in the software version. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_versions_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES service_providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerability_impact" (
  "impact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each impact entry */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system affected */,
  "impact_level" TEXT /* Impact level (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "impact_description" TEXT /* Description of the impact */,
  "impact_mitigation" TEXT /* Measures taken to mitigate the impact of the vulnerability. */,
  "impact_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the impact (e.g., short-term, long-term). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("impact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerability_impact_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerability_impact_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the system */,
  "security_compliance" TEXT /* Security compliance level of the system (e.g., PCI-DSS, HIPAA). */,
  "last_security_update" TEXT /* Date of the last security update for the system. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system version */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the system */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the system version */,
  "security_compliance" TEXT /* Security compliance level of the system version (e.g., PCI-DSS, HIPAA). */,
  "security_features" TEXT /* List of security features included in the system version. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_versions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_vulnerabilities" (
  "relation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship entry */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "affected" INTEGER /* Whether the system is affected by the vulnerability */,
  "patched" INTEGER /* Whether the system has been patched */,
  "change_password" INTEGER /* Whether users need to change their passwords */,
  "mitigation_status" TEXT /* Status of mitigation efforts (e.g., in progress, completed, pending). */,
  "mitigation_plan" TEXT /* Plan for mitigating the vulnerability. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_vulnerabilities_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_vulnerabilities_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_patches" (
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patch */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "vuln_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vulnerability */,
  "patch_date" TEXT /* Date the patch was applied */,
  "patch_version" TEXT /* Version of the patch */,
  "patch_description" TEXT /* Description of the patch */,
  "patch_effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of the patch (e.g., partial, full). */,
  "patch_tested" INTEGER /* Whether the patch has been tested. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_patches_vuln_id FOREIGN KEY ("vuln_id") REFERENCES vulnerabilities ("vuln_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_patches_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)
